# ZzFX Studio

> Algorithmic chiptune tracker that generates 4-channel retro songs instantly using pure math. Songs export as self-contained JavaScript using the ZzFXM sequencer format.

ZzFX Studio generates complete 4-channel songs (melody, harmony, bass, drums) with no AI -- pure algorithmic composition using scale-constrained note selection, Euclidean rhythms, and genre-aware templates.

Exported code from ZzFX Studio is ready to use in games, demos, and creative projects. There are two export formats: a full JS file and a compact one-liner.

## Using exported code

### Full JS export

The full export is a self-contained `.js` file that includes a minified player and song data. Drop it into any project and it plays immediately:

```js
// Generated by ZZFx Gen Studio
// @zzfx-studio {"v":3,"config":{...},...}

const zzfxR=44100,zzfxX=new AudioContext;function zzfxP(...){...}function zzfxG(...){...}function zzfxM(...){...}

const instruments = [
  [.8,.05,330,...],  // melody
  [.6,.1,220,...],   // harmony
  [1,.05,110,...],   // bass
  [.3,0,50,...],     // drums
];

const patterns = [
  [ // A (verse)
    [0,0,24,0,24,...],  // channel data: [instrument, panning, ...notes]
    [1,0,20,0,20,...],
    [2,0,12,0,12,...],
    [3,0,1,14,32,...],
  ],
];

const sequence = [0,0,1,0];
const BPM = 120;

// Requires user gesture (click/tap) -- AudioContext needs interaction to start
zzfxP(...zzfxM(instruments, patterns, sequence, BPM));
```

The `// @zzfx-studio` comment line contains full song JSON for lossless re-import back into the tracker.

### Compact one-liner

The clipboard export is a single `zzfxM()` call:

```js
zzfxM([[.8,.05,330,...],[.6,.1,220,...],[1,.05,110,...],[.3,0,50,...]],[[...channels...],...],sequence,BPM)
```

This requires a ZzFXM player to be available in scope.

## Playing with @zzfx-studio/zzfxm

For projects using npm, install the `@zzfx-studio/zzfxm` package instead of embedding the minified player:

```sh
npm install @zzfx-studio/zzfxm zzfx
```

```js
import { zzfxm, ZZFXM } from '@zzfx-studio/zzfxm';

// Build and play in one call
zzfxm(instruments, patterns, sequence, BPM);

// Or build sample data separately (useful for offline rendering, WAV export)
const [left, right] = ZZFXM.build(instruments, patterns, sequence, BPM);

// Then play when ready
ZZFXM.play([left, right]);
```

### Micro build (zero dependencies, ~1.4KB gzipped)

```sh
npm install @zzfx-studio/zzfxm
```

```js
import { zzfxm } from '@zzfx-studio/zzfxm/micro';

zzfxm(instruments, patterns, sequence, BPM);
```

The micro build bundles ZzFX inline. Use this for music-only playback. If you also need ZzFX for sound effects, install both packages separately to avoid bundling the synth twice.

## Song data format

```
instruments: number[][]   -- array of ZzFX sound parameter arrays (20 params each)
patterns:    number[][][] -- [pattern][channel][instrumentIndex, panning, ...32 notes]
sequence:    number[]     -- pattern indices defining playback order
BPM:         number       -- tempo (default: 125)
```

### Channels

Each song has 4 channels:
- Channel 0: Melody
- Channel 1: Harmony
- Channel 2: Bass
- Channel 3: Drums

### Notes

Notes are MIDI-style integers where 12 = the instrument's base frequency. A note value of 0 means silence/rest for that step. Fractional values (e.g. 12.5) control attenuation for fadeout effects.

### Drums

Drum channel uses a single noise instrument. Different note values produce different sounds:
- 1-6: Kick
- 7-22: Snare
- 23+: Hi-hat

### Panning

Channel panning is stored as the second value in each channel array. Range: -1 (full left) to 1 (full right), 0 = center.

## Song configuration

Songs are generated with these parameters:
- **vibe**: adventure, battle, dungeon, titleScreen, boss
- **key**: C, C#, D, D#, E, F, F#, G, G#, A, A#, B
- **scale**: major, minor, pentatonic, dorian, mixolydian, harmonicMinor
- **bpm**: tempo in beats per minute
- **length**: short, long, epic

## API reference

### zzfxm(instruments, patterns, sequence, BPM?)

Render and play a song via Web Audio. Returns an `AudioBufferSourceNode`.

### ZZFXM.build(instruments, patterns, sequence, BPM?)

Render a song to stereo sample data. Returns `[leftChannel, rightChannel]` as `number[]` arrays. No audio playback.

### ZZFXM.play(sampleChannels, volumeScale?, rate?, pan?, loop?)

Play stereo sample data via Web Audio. Returns an `AudioBufferSourceNode`.

- `volumeScale` (default: 1) -- volume multiplier
- `rate` (default: 1) -- playback rate
- `pan` (default: 0) -- stereo panning (-1 to 1)
- `loop` (default: false) -- loop playback

### ZZFXM.sampleRate

The sample rate used for synthesis: 44100.

## Important notes

- AudioContext requires a user gesture (click/tap) before audio can play in browsers.
- The `zzfx` npm package creates an AudioContext at import time. In Web Workers where AudioContext doesn't exist, stub it first: `globalThis.AudioContext = class {};`
- Songs exported from other ZzFXM tools may sound slightly different due to ZzFX synth version differences.

## Links

- [ZzFX Studio](https://thejustinwalsh.github.io/zzfx-studio) -- the tracker app
- [@zzfx-studio/zzfxm on npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@zzfx-studio/zzfxm) -- the player package
- [ZzFX](https://github.com/KilledByAPixel/ZzFX) -- the synth engine by Frank Force
- [ZzFXM](https://github.com/keithclark/ZzFXM) -- the original sequencer by Keith Clark
